Explanation: This is the exchange on which the symbol is trading
This is the last 2 digits of the year
Possible Values: 2019 will be 19; 2020 will be 20; 2021 will be 21; 2022 will be 22

Explanation: For monthly expiries such as futures and options, this value is the 3 characters of the month. The month will always be denoted in uppercase.
Possible Values: JAN FEB MAR APR MAY JUN JUL AUG SEP OCT NOV DEC

Explanation: For weekly expiries, this value represents the month of expiry but with only 1 character. This is a mix of numbers as well as characters.
Possible Values: Jan -> 1. Feb -> 2. Mar -> 3. Apr -> 4. May -> 5. Jun -> 6. Jul -> 7. Aug -> 8. Sep -> 9. Oct -> O (Letter). Nov -> N. Dec -> D

Explanation: This is used in weekly expiries. This represents the date of the month on which the contract is going to expire. This value will always be 2 characters.
This represents whether the contract is call or put
Possible Values: Call Option -> CE & Put Option -> PE

Explanation: This is the strike price of the option contract
